name: Shady Trade Deal
tips: The best discard target is Dark Matter Stash — you get Shady's gain AND Stash's on-discard gain, each worth +1 Dark Matter per friendly Dark Mage, for 1 mana total.
Discarding any card also enables Illicit Shadow Stones on your next turn (play condition: discarded last turn). Plan your sequencing across turns.

At only 1 mana, this is one of the cheapest actions in the game — an easy pick for your single action on a light-mana turn. Play a Dark Mage one turn, then follow up with Shady Trade Deal the next to immediately start building your pool.
Be careful what you discard — cards go to the exhaust pile, not the grave. Ratical Resurrection cannot bring back discarded cards.
This card is the engine of the Dark Matter archetype. Without it, pool growth is much slower — treat every copy as precious, and never spend it as discard fodder for another card.
rulings: To play this card, you must discard one other card from your hand (any card, any type). The discarded card goes to the exhaust pile.

You gain +1 Dark Matter per friendly [[Dark Mage]] on the board (element Dark AND the Mage tribe; composite tribes count since 2026-07-10). Dark Mages in hand or deck do not count. With zero Dark Mages on board, the gain is 0.
Dark Matter is a PLAYER pool — the gain credits your pool, not any minion. Both players can always see both pools.
The discarded card may trigger its own on-discard effects (e.g. Dark Matter Stash grants +1 Dark Matter per friendly Dark Mage when discarded).
If you have no other cards in hand, you cannot pay the discard cost and cannot play this card.
